title: blazewrit harness — Verify step structured_output
description: Verify = FLOW-LEVEL goal verifier (NOT a step reviewer; no reviewer of its own — internal multi-pass IS the quality gate). Runs in every flow. OUTPUT is a discriminated union on `result`: pass | degraded_pass | fail | blocked | retry_exhausted (HARNESS_FLOW_REVIEW G5 adds degraded_pass|blocked to the README's pass|fail|retry_exhausted for the tool-degrade case). TARGET RUNTIME: grammar-constrained generation — M1 enforces SHAPE/enum/required only. Truth-bearing fields (CountClaim value↔raw_stdout, VerifyProbe re-execution, sha256 re-hash, declared_next_step==expected_next_step, pyreez disagree==self-misjudgment) are closed by SIBLING M2 validator_contracts listed in this step's manifest, NOT by any keyword here. Mechanical-pass tool fields (typecheck/tests/firebat/emberdeck/pyreez) reuse the shared DegradableMeasurement (M3) + CountClaim (R23) because those tools can be ABSENT/FAIL/timeout — absence is a first-class schema BRANCH, never null/placeholder (R22).

SourceVersion.description: Freshness anchor for race detection / Stale-ED-2nd-attempt. Mirrors Ground.task_subgraph.ed_snapshot_version (a content hash). NOT a free-filled flow_id/verified_at envelope — it is the measured freshness hash the stale-2nd-attempt and race-detection logic compare against. Flow/step correlation otherwise lives in the top-level `based_on` RowRef bundle (§5).

ed_snapshot_version.description: The ed_snapshot_version hash carried from Ground.task_subgraph, re-checked for staleness/races.

EscalationCause.description: WHY Verify could not produce a clean producer-attributed verdict (blocked/retry_exhausted). Carries cap/self-misjudgment/gate/precondition semantics so the step-attribution failure_origin enum stays clean (field-type consistency). verify_self_misjudgment/gate_tool_unavailable/stale_2nd_attempt/input_precondition_fault belong to `blocked`; retry_cap/cap_exceeded belong to `retry_exhausted`.

P0PostStabilization.description: P0 (bugfix-p0) only, on result ∈ {pass, degraded_pass}. Verify SIGNALS a recommended follow-up when complexity_signal was high or a god_node was in scope; it does NOT queue/create the flow (orchestrator queues — Verify-signals-only boundary). Records the SUGGESTED follow-up. triggered_by is an ARRAY (both signals can fire independently — record every one that held). suggested_depth applies ONLY when suggested_followup=bugfix_normal (deep); for suggested_followup=retro it MUST be absent (depth is meaningless for a retro follow-up) — enforced by the allOf below.

allOf.$comment: suggested_depth is permitted only for the bugfix_normal follow-up; forbidden for retro.

Defect 4 — prose L309 says suggested_depth applies ONLY for suggested_followup=bugfix_normal and L308/L310 fix its value to 'deep'. The shared Depth enum {shallow,deep} otherwise permits suggested_depth=shallow on a bugfix_normal follow-up, contradicting prose. So on bugfix_normal: suggested_depth is REQUIRED and const 'deep'.

triggered_by.description: Which signal(s) fired the recommendation — an ARRAY because the two conditions are independent and can BOTH hold (Triage.complexity_signal=high AND god_nodes_in_scope non-empty). high_complexity = Triage.complexity_signal was high; god_node = Ground.task_subgraph.god_nodes_in_scope non-empty. Both-fired ⇒ both items present (order-insensitive, unique).

suggested_followup.description: The recommended follow-up flow (a SIGNAL — orchestrator decides actual queuing/execution).

suggested_depth.description: Recommended depth for the follow-up (deep when suggested_followup=bugfix_normal).

evidence.description: Refs backing the recommendation — an ARRAY of {trigger, ref} entries, one per fired triggered_by item (both-fired ⇒ both a god_node ref and a complexity ref are cited, single-fired ⇒ one). trigger names which signal the ref backs; ref is the god_node ref (trigger=god_node) or complexity ref (trigger=high_complexity). Cardinality/keying to triggered_by (one evidence entry per fired trigger) is an M2 validator_contract; the array shape is closed here.

trigger.description: Which fired signal this ref backs (mirrors a triggered_by item).

ref.description: god_node ref (trigger=god_node) or complexity ref (trigger=high_complexity) backing the recommendation.

result.description: DISCRIMINANT (top-level oneOf). pass = flow goal achieved, no tool degrade. degraded_pass = goal achieved but the pyreez cross-verify (ENHANCEMENT) leg was OMITTED (absent/error/timeout) so the verdict rests on the internal multi-pass alone (G5/P5); pyreez-specific — gate-tool failure does NOT produce degraded_pass. fail = a PRODUCER step produced a wrong result; routes via failure_origin (step-attribution only). blocked = Verify cannot produce its own verdict: gate-tool (firebat/emberdeck) mechanical error/timeout, stale-2nd-attempt, input-precondition fault, OR self-misjudgment (pyreez disagreement / adversarial-overturns / post_hoc_signal / unattributable_goal_failure) → carries the escalation object with failure_origin∈{verify,ground}. retry_exhausted = max iterations / cap hit → flow halt + escalate, Reflect classifies `abandoned` (NO DONE_WITH_CONCERNS type exists).

based_on.description: §5 cross-step refs (Postgres step_runs row ids, NEVER file paths). Verify reads the WHOLE flow chain, so it records the rows it evaluated. spec_ref/test_ref present only on code flows (FORBIDDEN on non-code via the top-level flow_kind allOf); report_ref present (and REQUIRED) only on non-code flows (FORBIDDEN on code) — Report is the non-code terminal producer anchor. triage_ref is the one always-present anchor (every flow starts at Triage).

triage_ref.description: Always present — every flow begins at Triage. Other *_ref keys (ground/investigate/decide/spec/test/report) come from the shared BasedOn bundle and are present per the flow chain.

flow_kind.description: Which internal multi-pass set Verify ran. 'code' = Mechanical/Goal-backward/Adversarial/pyreez (typecheck+tests+firebat+emberdeck). 'non_code' = Completeness/Goal-backward/Adversarial/pyreez. Drives which internal_passes branch is populated. REQUIRED on every branch EXCEPT the early-abort blocked branch (result=blocked + escalation.cause=input_precondition_fault + internal_passes.aborted=true), where the chain may be malformed/unreadable and Verify cannot honestly classify flow_kind — there it is conditionally-not-required (NOT defaulted to a fabricated value), and its pass1/based_on couplings are correspondingly lifted. That conditional requirement lives in the top-level allOf.

high_risk_flow.description: Whether this flow is high-risk (triggers pyreez cross-verification on Pass 2-3, R5). Mirrors Triage/Decide risk. Used to decide whether pyreez_cross_verification is expected vs legitimately Omitted(not_applicable).

internal_passes.description: The internal multi-pass record (Verify's self-verification — there is NO external reviewer). Exactly the four passes for the flow_kind. Pass 1 differs by flow_kind; Passes 2/3/4 are shared in shape. pyreez (Pass 4) and the code-mechanical tools are tool-dependent → DegradableMeasurement. The populated pass2/3/4 record is required on EVERY branch EXCEPT the early-abort blocked branch (result=blocked + escalation.cause=input_precondition_fault), where Verify bails BEFORE the passes run and instead sets aborted=true — that conditional requirement lives in the top-level allOf, not here (so the not-run case is not forced to fabricate pass records).

aborted.description: true ⇒ the passes did NOT run because Verify bailed on an input-precondition fault before executing them (result=blocked + escalation.cause=input_precondition_fault). In that case pass2/3/4 populated records are not required. Absent/false ⇒ the passes ran and the full 4-pass record is required (enforced in the top-level allOf).

pass1_mechanical.description: CODE-FLOW Pass 1. typecheck + all tests pass + firebat blockers=0 + emberdeck drift=0. EVERY sub-tool is degradable (MCP/CLI can be absent/fail) → DegradableMeasurement, and the counts (tests passed/failed, firebat blockers, emberdeck drift) are CountClaim (R23). Present iff flow_kind='code'.

allOf.$comment: Defect 8 — mechanical_fallback_used=true is not free-fillable: it asserts a gate sub-tool was construction-ABSENT and the leg fell back to typecheck+tests. So flag=true REQUIRES at least one of firebat/emberdeck to be its Omitted branch (status:'omitted'); otherwise (both Measured) no fallback actually occurred and the flag is forbidden-true. M1-expressible (each sub-tool's Omitted arm has required:['status'] with status const 'omitted'), so closed in grammar rather than left an unverifiable audit flag. flag absent/false is unconstrained.

mechanical_fallback_used.description: true ⇒ a gate sub-tool (firebat/emberdeck) was construction-ABSENT so the mechanical leg fell back to typecheck+tests (firebat.md/emberdeck.md degrade). The absent sub-tool itself is recorded as its Omitted branch; this flag marks that the fallback path decided the mechanical verdict. Optional (absent ⇒ no fallback was used). COUPLED (allOf above): flag=true REQUIRES at least one of firebat/emberdeck to be its Omitted branch — it cannot be set true when both gate tools are Measured (no fallback actually occurred).

typecheck.description: Typecheck result. Degradable (no typecheck command in project ⇒ Omitted{skipped}). Measured.value carries pass/fail + the error CountClaim.

errors.description: Count of type errors (R23 — measured count, never a bare integer).

tests.description: Test suite result. Degradable (no test runner ⇒ Omitted). Measured.value carries all_pass + passed/failed CountClaims.

firebat.description: firebat code-quality scan. MCP-backed → degradable (firebat not attached ⇒ Omitted{unavailable}). PASS requires blockers=0; blockers is a CountClaim (R23).

blockers.description: firebat blocker count — PASS iff 0 (R23 CountClaim).

emberdeck.description: emberdeck (ED) intent↔code drift check. MCP graph-backed → degradable (ED unavailable/stale ⇒ Omitted). PASS requires drift=0; drift is a CountClaim (R23).

drift.description: emberdeck intent↔code drift count — PASS iff 0 (R23 CountClaim).

pass1_completeness.description: NON-CODE-FLOW Pass 1. required items present + evidence cited + measurements exist. Present iff flow_kind='non_code'.

missing_items.description: Required items the output lacks (each sourced). Empty ⇒ complete.

pass2_goal_backward.description: Pass 2 (both flow_kinds). Traces original request → plan → tests → code (code) / request → output (non-code): 'what must be TRUE'. Each traced assertion is a finding carrying its provenance + an optional re-runnable VerifyProbe (R20 — TRUTH is the re-execution, an M2 validator_contract).

assertion.description: A 'what must be TRUE' statement derived from the original request.

evidence_ref.description: Optional file:line or artifact pointer backing this assertion. A RowRef when it points at an upstream step output (§5); plain string for a code file:line citation.

verify_probe.description: Optional re-runnable proof (R20). SHAPE here; re-execution vs expected_result is an M2 validator_contract.

pass3_adversarial.description: Pass 3 (both flow_kinds). 'How could this still fail? What did I miss?' / 'this conclusion could be wrong because...'. Surfaces residual failure modes; if any has the weight to OVERTURN Pass 1 mechanical, that is a self-misjudgment signal (see self_misjudgment_check).

overturns_pass1.description: true = an adversarial finding here is strong enough to REVERSE the Pass 1 mechanical verdict → a self-misjudgment trigger (R2).

pass4_pyreez_cross_verification.description: Pass 4 (both flow_kinds, R5). pyreez multi-model cross-verification for high-risk flows. TOOL-DEPENDENT (pyreez can be absent) → DegradableMeasurement, but the Omitted arm is TIGHTENED to the 5 pyreez-legal reasons {tool_absent,tool_failed,timeout,unavailable,not_applicable} — 'skipped' is excluded (Ground 'command-not-defined', not applicable to pyreez), single-sourced with degraded_tools.omission. Omitted{unavailable} when pyreez not attached, Omitted{not_applicable} on a non-high-risk flow. Measured.value carries the CrossVerifyFlag result; cross_verify_result='disagree' (opposite verdict) is the self-misjudgment trigger (R2/R5) → result=blocked with escalation.failure_origin=verify (NOT a fail — pyreez disagreement makes Verify distrust its OWN verdict, so it blocks rather than attributing to a producer). pyreez ABSENCE (not disagreement) drives degraded_pass instead.

value.description: pyreez cross-verification outcome (R5 CrossVerifyFlag). cross_verify_required=true on high-risk; cross_verify_result='disagree' = opposite verdict from primary passes = self-misjudgment.

cross_verify_required.description: R5 trigger (from shared CrossVerifyFlag). true on a high-risk flow.

cross_verify_result.description: pyreez verdict vs primary passes. 'disagree' = opposite verdict = self-misjudgment signal (R2) → blocked{escalation.failure_origin:verify}. 'agree'/'inconclusive' = cross-verify leg passes (normal aggregation). 'not_run' = pyreez was PRESENT but produced NO cross-verify result (leg did not complete) — distinct from tool ABSENCE (which is the Omitted arm, not Measured). Its aggregation verdict is the SAME degrade-meaning as absence (md §pyreez table `present`+`not_run` row + Multi-pass aggregation rule 6): on a high-risk flow → result=degraded_pass with a degraded_tools entry reason=tool_failed (present-but-no-result), pass4 staying Measured{not_run} to preserve the present fact; on a non-high-risk flow → result=pass (leg not forced). NOT a self-misjudgment (only 'disagree' is). The realized-result mapping (cross_verify_result=not_run ⇒ degrade vs pass by high_risk_flow) is an M2 validator_contract — grammar cannot express the cross-field verdict, but the verdict is no longer silent. From shared CrossVerifyFlag.

oneOf.$comment: pyreez omitted arm — Omitted-shaped but reason TIGHTENED to the 5 values legal for pyreez (excludes 'skipped', which is Ground 'command-not-defined' and NOT applicable to pyreez), single-sourcing the pyreez omission reason vocabulary with degraded_tools.omission. Canonical mapping: absent→tool_absent, error→tool_failed, tool_unavailable→unavailable, tool_timeout→timeout, plus not_applicable (non-high-risk).

self_misjudgment_check.description: R2 self-misjudgment detector. Verify suspects its OWN verdict when: (a) pyreez cross-verify gives the OPPOSITE verdict; (b) a Pass 3 adversarial finding overturns Pass 1 mechanical; (c) a post-hoc external signal (user reject / downstream failure); (d) an unattributable_goal_failure (diffuse defect with no step locus). `suspected` records this R2 truth on EVERY output (top-level required). The REALIZED result follows Trigger precedence: a higher-precedence trigger (retry_exhausted, or a non-self-misjudgment blocked cause — input_precondition_fault/gate_tool_unavailable/stale_2nd_attempt) can co-fire and win, so suspected=true does NOT force result=blocked in that case (it stays honestly true while the realized result follows precedence). The grammar coupling fires only on the REALIZED self-misjudgment block: result=blocked ∧ escalation.cause=verify_self_misjudgment ⇒ escalation.failure_origin='verify' (escalate=true / NEEDS_CONTEXT / no auto-reinvoke — prevents infinite self-route). Self-misjudgment is NOT a `fail` (a fail attributes to a producer step); Verify distrusting its own verdict cannot honestly attribute to a producer, so it blocks. The residual suspected⇒(result,cause) realization under precedence is an M2 validator_contract. This object records WHY (auditable), not the routing.

suspected.description: true ⇒ Verify distrusts its own verdict (R2 condition is true, recorded honestly). The realized result follows Trigger precedence: absent a higher-precedence trigger this becomes result=blocked with escalation.cause=verify_self_misjudgment + failure_origin='verify' (escalate=true, NEEDS_CONTEXT, no auto-reinvoke); if a higher-precedence trigger co-fired (retry_exhausted / other blocked cause), suspected stays true while the realized result follows precedence (M2 validator_contract).

triggers.description: Which R2 conditions fired. Present (non-empty) iff suspected=true; forbidden when suspected=false — coupled in the top-level allOf so a suspected=true output always carries the auditable WHY and a suspected=false output cannot carry stale triggers.

declared_next_step.description: R16 ADVISORY successor declaration. Verify does NOT free-fill the authoritative next step — the orchestrator INJECTS expected_next_step and reads `result`/`failure_origin` to route. declared_next_step==expected_next_step is an M2 validator_contract, not enforced here.

$comment: DISCRIMINATED UNION on `result`. The top-level oneOf above guarantees EXACTLY ONE `result` const is chosen (grammar enforces a single valid branch). Each branch's PAYLOAD property schema AND its per-branch `required` are co-located in the allOf if/then block below (so a required property is always defined in the same schema object — satisfies strict validators). When an if matches, its then-properties count as evaluated and unevaluatedProperties:false does not reject them; payloads from the WRONG branch are unevaluated and therefore rejected.

failure.description: FAIL payload. Routing record the orchestrator reads. A `fail` attributes the defect to a PRODUCER step (triage..report or 'multiple'); it NEVER carries 'verify' (self-misjudgment is `blocked`, not fail) and NEVER 'cap_exceeded' (cap is `retry_exhausted`). Flow correlation is the top-level `based_on` RowRef bundle — this payload does not free-fill flow_id/schema_version/verified_at (§5).

failure_origin.description: WHICH PRODUCER step the failure is attributed to. STEP-ATTRIBUTION enum only — a strict subset of _defs FailureOrigin that EXCLUDES the control values 'verify' (self-misjudgment routes to `blocked`) and 'cap_exceeded' (routes to `retry_exhausted`). cap/self-misjudgment causes are carried by the escalation/exhaustion `cause` field, NOT by polluting this step-attribution enum (field-type consistency). 'multiple' = several bad steps (route earliest first). 'report' valid ONLY on non-code flows — forbidden on flow_kind=code via the top-level allOf (homologous to the report_ref-forbidden-on-code constraint). Drives the produce⇄review re-entry table.

evidence.description: file:line or artifact reference backing the failure. RowRef when pointing at an upstream step output (§5); plain string for a code file:line.

escalate.description: true ⇒ NEEDS_CONTEXT escalation, NO auto-reinvoke. For a fail this is normally false (the orchestrator re-enters the attributed producer's produce⇄review loop). An M2 validator_contract asserts the routing coupling.

source_version.description: Freshness correlation — Ground.task_subgraph.ed_snapshot_version mirrored for race detection (homologous to pass/blocked source_version). Optional.

earliest_step.description: When failure_origin='multiple', the earliest problematic step to route to first (shared StepName enum). Required by the M2 validator when failure_origin='multiple'.

reclassify_count.description: When failure_origin='triage', how many reclassify cycles have occurred (cap 3). An internal orchestrator-tracked cycle counter — a plain bounded integer, NOT a CountClaim (there is no command/stdout to hash).

verdict_summary.description: Human-readable statement of WHAT was verified true.

degraded_tools.description: G5. The degraded_pass branch is PYREEZ-SPECIFIC: it is reached ONLY when the pyreez cross-verify leg was OMITTED (the enhancement tool was absent/error/timeout) and all other passes passed (Multi-pass aggregation rule 3). Gate-tool (firebat/emberdeck) mechanical error routes to `blocked`, not here; their construction-absence is a fallback INSIDE the mechanical leg, not a degraded_pass driver. So `tool` is restricted to pyreez. Each entry names the tool and the Omitted reason. MUST be non-empty.

omission.description: The Omitted record for the degraded pyreez leg. Shaped like shared _defs/Omitted but the reason is TIGHTENED to the 4 values legal for a degraded pyreez leg — `skipped` (Ground 'command not defined') is NOT applicable to pyreez and is excluded, and `not_applicable` is excluded too (a non-high-risk flow with pyreez absent routes to result=pass, NOT degraded_pass — the degraded_pass branch is only reached on a high-risk forced-pyreez absence/error/timeout, so the not_applicable case never appears here; it lives only in the pass4_pyreez_cross_verification Omitted arm). Canonical reason mapping: absent→tool_absent, error→tool_failed, tool_unavailable→unavailable, tool_timeout→timeout. reason ∈ {tool_absent, tool_failed, timeout, unavailable}. The mandatory_but_unavailable case is recorded separately via mandatory_but_unavailable=true (high-risk flow whose forced pyreez was absent — verdict still degraded_pass). SINGLE-SOURCE M2 validator_contract — the degraded_pass branch has TWO distinct sub-cases, each with its own consistency contract: (A) pyreez ABSENT/error/timeout sub-case — pass4 is the Omitted arm, and this omission and that SAME invocation's internal_passes.pass4_pyreez_cross_verification Omitted arm record the ONE SAME pyreez absence, so their `reason` MUST be EQUAL (pass4.Omitted.reason == degraded_tools[].omission.reason). (B) present-but-no-result (Measured{not_run}) sub-case — pass4 is the Measured arm (cross_verify_result=not_run), which has NO `.reason` field, so the (A) reason-equality is structurally inapplicable; instead the M2 validator requires degraded_tools[].omission.reason == 'tool_failed' AND pass4.value.cross_verify_result == 'not_run' (the present-but-no-result leg is normalized into the omission shape with reason=tool_failed — see md §pyreez table `present`+`not_run` row). Grammar cannot const-bind the equality of two independent objects' reasons (the pass4 Omitted arm additionally allows not_applicable, which never co-occurs with degraded_pass), nor branch the (A)/(B) sub-case on the pass4 arm tag, so each sub-case's mirror is closed by the M2 validator — a producer MUST NOT emit pass4 Omitted{tool_absent} alongside degraded_tools{timeout} (contradictory absence reasons), NOR pass4 Measured{not_run} alongside degraded_tools{omission.reason≠tool_failed}.

mandatory_but_unavailable.description: MUST be true on every degraded_tools entry. The degraded_pass branch is reachable ONLY on a high-risk forced-pyreez absence/error/timeout (a low-risk legitimately-omitted leg routes to result=pass, not here), so every entry is by construction the mandatory-but-unavailable case — equivalent to high_risk_flow=true (a separate top-level allOf binds result=degraded_pass ⇒ high_risk_flow=true). Mandatory means try-obligation, not success-precondition (P5); the verdict is degraded_pass.

source_version.description: Freshness anchor mirrored from Ground.task_subgraph.ed_snapshot_version (race detection). Optional.

p0_post_stabilization.$comment: Shared with the PASS branch — P0 follow-up SIGNAL is emitted on result ∈ {pass, degraded_pass}.

verdict_summary.description: Human-readable statement of WHAT was verified true.

escalation.description: BLOCKED payload — Verify cannot produce its own verdict. Covers ALL non-clean-fail blocked causes: self-misjudgment, gate-tool mechanical error/timeout, stale-2nd-attempt, input-precondition fault. blocked is ALWAYS NEEDS_CONTEXT escalation with NO auto-reinvoke — this disposition is a STRUCTURAL CONSTANT of the blocked branch, so (unlike the sibling `failure` and `exhaustion` payloads, whose escalate flag VARIES — fail is normally escalate=false re-entering the producer loop, exhaustion is const true) there is NO recordable escalate/disposition field here: every blocked is needs-context by construction, the routing follows from cause/failure_origin alone. Flow correlation is the top-level `based_on` RowRef bundle (§5) — this object does not free-fill flow_id/schema_version/verified_at.

cause.description: Why Verify is blocked. blocked uses {verify_self_misjudgment, gate_tool_unavailable, stale_2nd_attempt, input_precondition_fault}. The cap causes (retry_cap/cap_exceeded) belong to `retry_exhausted`, not here.

failure_origin.description: STEP-ID locus of the block — ONLY {verify, ground}: verify = self-misjudgment (pyreez disagreement / adversarial-overturns / post_hoc_signal / unattributable_goal_failure); ground = stale_2nd_attempt. Omitted for gate_tool_unavailable / input_precondition_fault (no producer-step attribution). REQUIRED + const-bound by cause via the top-level allOf: cause=verify_self_misjudgment ⇒ failure_origin='verify', cause=stale_2nd_attempt ⇒ failure_origin='ground'. Never a cause value (field-type consistency).

reason.description: Specific block reason (e.g. firebat_unavailable, upstream_chain_malformed, invalid_flow_type, unattributable_goal_failure).

evidence.description: file:line or artifact reference backing the block. RowRef when pointing at an upstream step output (§5).

source_version.description: Freshness anchor — required-in-spirit for stale_2nd_attempt (the stale hash that triggered the block). Optional otherwise.

blocked_on.description: G5. For cause=gate_tool_unavailable: the verdict-blocking gate tool(s) (firebat/emberdeck) and why their absence cannot be degraded around. Empty/absent for non-tool block causes.

tool.description: Only the verdict-blocking GATE tools (firebat/emberdeck). typecheck/tests degrade via the mechanical fallback and pyreez drives degraded_pass — none of those are gate_tool_unavailable blockers.

why_verdict_blocking.description: Why a degraded_pass is NOT acceptable without this tool (the verdict structurally depends on it).

exhaustion.description: RETRY_EXHAUSTED payload → flow halt + escalate, Reflect → `abandoned`. NO DONE_WITH_CONCERNS. Flow correlation is the top-level `based_on` RowRef bundle (§5) — not free-filled flow_id/verified_at.

cause.description: WHY the flow exhausted — the cap that was hit. retry_exhausted uses {retry_cap, cap_exceeded}. cap causes are not attributed to a single producer step, so failure_origin is omitted; the cap meaning is carried HERE (field-type consistency).

iterations.description: How many produce⇄review iterations were attempted before exhaustion. An internal orchestrator-tracked counter — a plain bounded integer (cap typically 5), NOT a CountClaim (no command/stdout to hash).

last_failure_origin.description: Optional — the STEP-ATTRIBUTION origin of the final failed iteration (the producer that kept failing). Omitted for cap_exceeded (global cap, no single producer). Same step-attribution enum as fail.failure_origin (no verify/cap_exceeded pollution). When 'multiple' (the last iteration's failure spanned several steps), earliest_step disambiguates the left-most problematic step for routing/learning — mirroring the fail payload.

earliest_step.description: When last_failure_origin='multiple', the earliest (left-most, chain-order) problematic step the multi-step last failure disambiguates to (shared StepName enum). Required by the M2 validator when last_failure_origin='multiple' (homologous to fail.earliest_step) so a multi-step last-iteration record is never unrecoverable for routing/learning.

escalate.description: MUST be true — retry_exhausted always halts + escalates; silent proceed is forbidden.

reflect_classification.description: MUST be 'abandoned' — the only Reflect classification for an exhausted flow (no DONE_WITH_CONCERNS).

allOf.$comment: Populated 4-pass record is required UNLESS this is the early-abort blocked branch (result=blocked + escalation.cause=input_precondition_fault + internal_passes.aborted=true), where Verify bailed before the passes ran. The `not` matches every case except that exact abort shape; in all matched cases the populated pass2/3/4 record is required. On the abort branch the passes are not required (aborted=true marks the not-run record honestly).

based_on flow-conditional ref presence (grammar-enforced — prose declared it but it was unencoded). flow_kind=code ⇒ report_ref forbidden (Report is a non-code-only step); flow_kind=non_code ⇒ spec_ref and test_ref forbidden (Spec/Test are code-only steps) AND report_ref REQUIRED (Report is the non-code terminal producer anchor — the non-code chain terminates at Report, so its row is the guaranteed terminal correlation anchor; asymmetric with the code-side report_ref-forbidden constraint). Key-forbidding and key-requiring are both M1-expressible, so closed here rather than left advisory. EARLY-ABORT EXEMPTION: on the abort branch (result=blocked + escalation.cause=input_precondition_fault + internal_passes.aborted=true) the upstream chain is precisely what is malformed/missing, so the non_code report_ref-required / spec_ref+test_ref-forbidden coupling is lifted (the chain just declared malformed cannot be forced to carry a terminal-anchor row — only triage_ref is guaranteed); the inner non_code `if` carries a nested not-aborted guard. The code-side report_ref-forbidden `then` is harmless on abort (forbidding a key is always satisfiable) so it is left unguarded.

failure_origin='report' is forbidden on a code flow (Report is a non-code-only step), homologous to the based_on.report_ref-forbidden-on-code constraint above and equally M1-expressible. flow_kind=code ⇒ failure.failure_origin MUST NOT be 'report'. (result=fail is the only case carrying `failure`; on non-fail results `failure` is absent so this `if` is vacuous there.)

self_misjudgment_check coupling (R2), GATED on trigger precedence. self_misjudgment_check.suspected is a top-level REQUIRED field that records the R2 condition TRUTHFULLY on every output; but the REALIZED result follows Trigger precedence (md §Trigger precedence): a higher-precedence trigger (retry_exhausted rule 1, or a non-self-misjudgment blocked cause — input_precondition_fault/gate_tool_unavailable/stale_2nd_attempt) can co-fire and win, in which case the realized result is NOT the self-misjudgment block. So the coupling binds ONLY when the realized block IS the self-misjudgment one: result=blocked AND escalation.cause=verify_self_misjudgment ⇒ escalation.failure_origin MUST be verify. When a higher-precedence trigger fired, suspected=true is still emitted honestly and this `if` does not match (so it is not rejected). The residual suspected⇒(result,cause) realization under precedence is an M2 validator_contract, not an M1 const binding (precedence is cross-trigger truth grammar cannot express).

stale_2nd_attempt routing coupling (M1, homologous to the verify_self_misjudgment const binding above). A stale-2nd block is a fixed routing fact: result=blocked AND escalation.cause=stale_2nd_attempt ⇒ escalation.failure_origin MUST be 'ground' (the stale ED is attributed to Ground). Grammar-expressible (const binding), so closed here rather than left optional/M2.

self_misjudgment_check.triggers presence coupling (M1). triggers is present (minItems 1) iff suspected=true, and forbidden when suspected=false. Closes the 'only present when suspected=true' contract in grammar (homologous to the suspected/verify coupling above): a suspected=true output must carry the auditable WHY, and a suspected=false output cannot carry stale triggers.

flow_kind ⇒ which Pass-1 variant is required (grammar-enforced). code ⇒ pass1_mechanical; non_code ⇒ pass1_completeness. The OTHER variant stays absent (additionalProperties:false on internal_passes already forbids unknown keys, and the unused variant being absent is correct). EARLY-ABORT EXEMPTION: when result=blocked + escalation.cause=input_precondition_fault + internal_passes.aborted=true, the passes (incl. pass1) did not run, so the flow_kind⇒pass1 coupling is lifted (the `if` requires aborted to be absent/non-true via the nested not).

flow_kind is top-level REQUIRED on every branch EXCEPT the early-abort blocked branch (result=blocked + escalation.cause=input_precondition_fault + internal_passes.aborted=true), where the chain may be malformed/unreadable so Verify cannot honestly classify flow_kind and MUST NOT fabricate a default (garbage-in is not coerced to a concrete classification). The `not` matches every case except that exact abort shape; in all matched cases flow_kind is required. On the abort branch flow_kind is optional (and if present must still be code|non_code per the property schema). Homologous to the internal_passes/pass1/based_on early-abort exemptions.

early-abort ⇒ self_misjudgment_check.suspected MUST be false (R2 not evaluable — no pass ran), which by the triggers presence coupling also forbids `triggers`. On result=blocked + escalation.cause=input_precondition_fault + internal_passes.aborted=true, no pass executed so a self-misjudgment cannot honestly be suspected; fabricating suspected=true on a not-run record is forbidden.

blocked_on presence coupling (M1). result=blocked AND escalation.cause=gate_tool_unavailable ⇒ escalation.blocked_on REQUIRED (minItems 1) — the auditable record of WHICH gate (firebat/emberdeck) blocked the verdict and why it could not be degraded around. Mirrors the verify_self_misjudgment/stale_2nd_attempt cause⇒field const-couplings. For other blocked causes blocked_on is absent (the array is gate-tool-specific).

degraded_pass is reachable ONLY on a high-risk forced-pyreez absence/error/timeout (a low-risk legitimately-omitted leg routes to result=pass with pass4 Omitted{not_applicable}). So result=degraded_pass ⇒ high_risk_flow=true — making high_risk_flow REQUIRED and const true on this branch, consistent with every degraded_tools entry carrying mandatory_but_unavailable=true. Binds the branch's reachability rationale into grammar.

EscalationCause partition closed in M1 (prose L267/L286 + the cause field descriptions). The shared 6-value EscalationCause is $ref'd by BOTH escalation.cause (blocked) and exhaustion.cause (retry_exhausted) for field-type consistency; these two if/then blocks NARROW it per branch so grammar forbids the cross-product. result=blocked ⇒ escalation.cause ∈ the 4 block causes {verify_self_misjudgment,gate_tool_unavailable,stale_2nd_attempt,input_precondition_fault}; result=retry_exhausted ⇒ exhaustion.cause ∈ the 2 cap causes {retry_cap,cap_exceeded}.

Defect 2 — failure_origin attribution is OMITTED for non-attributing block causes (prose L268). failure_origin is const-coupled to verify (cause=verify_self_misjudgment) and ground (cause=stale_2nd_attempt) above; for gate_tool_unavailable/input_precondition_fault there is NO producer-step attribution, so failure_origin must be forbidden — otherwise grammar would permit e.g. cause=gate_tool_unavailable + failure_origin=verify, an attribution prose forbids.

Defect 5 / robustness — input_precondition_fault FORCES the early-abort bail state. Prose L69 says Verify bails BEFORE the passes execute on an input-precondition fault, and the entire early-abort exemption machinery (4-pass / flow_kind / based_on / suspected couplings) keys on the exact triple result=blocked + escalation.cause=input_precondition_fault + internal_passes.aborted=true. Nothing otherwise ties cause=input_precondition_fault to aborted=true, so a producer could emit the fault WITHOUT aborted and re-trigger the full populated-record requirement. This binds internal_passes.aborted const true on that cause so the bail state is grammar-forced (matching prose L69 'passes 돌기 전 bail').
